title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy scores winning goal. And then it was time to see the dentist — again.
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Charlie McAvoy scored the winning goal for the Bruins in a 2-1 overtime win against the Los Angeles Kings.
McAvoy's face was injured during the game against Kings, requiring further dental work after a previous slap shot injury.
McAvoy's performance and resilience are notable as the Bruins are without a captain, with him among the alternates.

title: Jaylen Brown blasts refs on social media after being ejected from Spurs game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown ejected after two technical fouls in Celtics' loss to Spurs, contributing eight points in 15 minutes.
Brown criticized referees Tyler Ford and Suyash Mehta on social media post-ejection, echoing previous commentary on officiating.
Celtics felt absence in 125-116 loss; Brown's second career ejection, team also missing Payton Pritchard and Nikola Vucevic.

title: Man sentenced for fatally stabbing Chelsea woman, 59
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Angel Alvarez, 68, was sentenced to life in prison for fatally stabbing former partner Margarita Morehead in Chelsea in 2023.
Alvarez pleaded guilty to second-degree murder, assault with a dangerous weapon, and violating a protection order issued in October 2022.
Morehead's death followed an argument on May 8, 2023; Alvarez attacked and fled despite a restraining order against him.

title: MBTA police seeking suspect who allegedly spat on, hit rider
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: MBTA Police seek public's help identifying suspect in Feb. 27 assault at Prudential station.
Suspect allegedly spat on and punched Green Line rider multiple times at 8 p.m.
Suspect described as wearing black clothes, with short hair, beard, tattoos on neck and hands.

title: Maine man allegedly shot and killed girlfriend; pair worked together at ski resort
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Austin Doucette, 24, charged with murder for allegedly shooting girlfriend Makayla Rose DeSantis, 23, in Carrabassett Valley, Maine.
DeSantis was shot at a condo complex rented to Sugarloaf Mountain Ski Resort employees; she died from her injuries.
A GoFundMe for DeSantis's burial collected over $10,000; the couple had been together for seven years.

title: Patriots reportedly remain interested in A.J. Brown trade after signing Romeo Doubs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots signed Romeo Doubs to a four-year contract worth up to $80 million, enhancing their receiver lineup.
Despite Doubs' addition, New England maintains interest in trading for Eagles' receiver A.J. Brown per reports.
The Eagles set a high trade price for Brown, possibly a 2027 first-round and a 2026 second-round pick.

title: She’s running the Boston Marathon to support breast cancer patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sydney Weisman, 24, from Fort Lauderdale, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in support of breast cancer patients.
Weisman is running with Team Ellie to honor her mother's breast cancer journey and the support from the Ellie Fund.
The Ellie Fund supports breast cancer patients with services like transportation, meals, childcare, and daily support.

title: 3 Stop & Shop locations in Boston, Reading receive bomb threats, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Police investigated bomb threats at Stop & Shop locations in Jamaica Plain, Allston, and Reading in Massachusetts.
Bomb squads dispatched found no credible threats at the three grocery store locations on Monday.
The threats, made within 15 minutes, are under investigation to determine if they are linked.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots WR Romeo Doubs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots sign WR Romeo Doubs to a four-year, $68 million contract from Packers, addressing roster changes after cutting Stefon Diggs.
Doubs, 26, excels in short-yardage and red-zone situations, catching 10 of 16 red-zone targets for scores last season.
Despite no 100-yard regular-season games, Doubs has two 100+ yard postseason games, totaling 371 yards in four playoff appearances.

title: She’s running the Boston Marathon for suicide prevention after the loss of 2 friends
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Kerrie Jacoby, 34, from Wellesley, Mass., is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for suicide prevention.
Jacoby is honoring friends Tyler and Michelle, both lost to suicide, aiming to raise mental health awareness.
She supports Samaritans, a suicide prevention group, highlighting the marathon's timing in April, when Tyler died.
